concepts.site_goal: Provide taxonomists, marine researchers, and conservationists with a dense, authoritative single-source reference for species taxonomy, habitat distribution, peer-reviewed literature, and conservation metrics
concepts.description: OceanIndex is a high-density institutional reference portal built on a strict blue-and-white grid. A three-panel taxonomy browser anchors the experience: a collapsible hierarchical tree on the left, a paginated species roster in the center, and a specimen detail panel on the right rail that slides in-place on click. Every page follows the same disciplined grid, monospace scientific annotations, and deep-sea blue (#003366) header band. Navigation is multi-level with persistent breadcrumbs. Data-heavy pages use sortable tables, faceted filters, and inline sparklines rather than decorative chrome.
concepts.motif: Institutional deep-sea blue (#003366) header band on crisp white content panels; monospace scientific annotations for measurements and taxonomy ranks; strict 12-column grid with dense gutters; tabular data-first layout; muted teal accent for interactive affordances

pages.layout_pattern: hero split: left two-thirds shows rotating featured-species spotlight with large binomial name, phylum badge, and IUCN status pill; right third houses a persistent taxonomy quick-search widget with autocomplete; below the fold a three-column recently-added species card grid with thumbnail silhouette, common name, and date-added stamp

three-panel: 240px sticky left sidebar contains collapsible taxonomy tree from kingdom to species with node counts; 60% center column shows paginated species list rows (thumbnail, binomial, authority, habitat zone, IUCN status pill); 280px right rail holds the specimen detail panel that slides in on row click — panel contains morphology measurements, synonyms, depth range bar, and classification breadcrumb

full-width SVG world map occupying the upper 55% of viewport with clickable ocean-basin regions rendered as colored polygons; below the map: a horizontal depth-zone filter strip (epipelagic, mesopelagic, bathypelagic toggles); right-side legend panel lists active species overlays with color swatches and species counts; clicking a basin polygon filters an inline result tally beneath the filter strip

220px left filter sidebar with faceted controls for year range slider, taxonomy phylum checkboxes, journal name multi-select, and open-access toggle; center region renders a sortable dense table with columns for title, lead author, journal, year, citation count badge, and a collapsible abstract row that expands inline; top bar shows active-filter chip row and result count

dashboard grid: top row spans full width with an IUCN status distribution bar chart showing species counts per category; middle section is a card matrix where each card represents a taxonomic order with its dominant status, population trend arrow, and a sparkline of assessment history; bottom section contains per-class trend line charts with a year-range selector

masonry card grid (3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile) where each card shows a specimen silhouette placeholder at top, common name in bold, scientific binomial in italics, a taxonomic rank badge (e.g., ORDER: Carcharhiniformes), habitat zone tag chips, maximum recorded length, and depth range data strip at card foot

message_intent: establish portal as the authoritative dense-reference destination for working marine scientists
enable rapid lookup and side-by-side comparison of species taxonomic data
surface geographic distribution patterns through layered habitat map overlays
communicate conservation urgency through dashboard-style status metrics and trend sparklines
content_model: species taxonomy records with full classification hierarchy from kingdom to subspecies
habitat zone polygon geodata with depth range and temperature band attributes
peer-reviewed publication metadata including DOI, abstract snippet, citation count, and taxa tags
IUCN Red List conservation status records with population trend and assessment year
field guide entries with morphological measurements, diagnostic characters, and behavioral notes
interactions: click species row in taxonomy browser to slide open specimen detail panel in right rail
click ocean zone polygon on habitat map to filter species list to that zone
sort publications table by year, citation count, or journal impact factor
toggle species taxon overlays on habitat map via checkbox legend
expand and collapse taxonomy tree nodes from phylum to species level

animations.description: Clicking a row in the center species list triggers the specimen detail panel in the right rail to animate from its collapsed off-canvas position (CSS transform: translateX(100%)) to its fully visible in-rail position (transform: translateX(0)) over 600ms with an ease-out cubic-bezier curve. The panel is already present in the DOM and absolutely positioned within the fixed-width right rail container, so no surrounding columns shift and no page scroll is triggered. The panel stays fully within the right rail viewport region for the entire 600ms transition.

asset_needs: specimen silhouette SVG icons keyed to taxonomic class (e.g., Chondrichthyes, Actinopterygii, Cetacea)
ocean zone vector base layer with basin and depth-zone polygon topology
IUCN status color-token palette (LC green through EX black)
monospace typeface for scientific annotations (e.g., IBM Plex Mono)
concepts.mobile_behavior: collapsible sidebar navigation behind hamburger; taxonomy tree stacks above species list; specimen detail panel becomes full-width bottom drawer; publications table switches to stacked card list

concepts.site_goal: Serve marine conservation officers, university researchers, and policy analysts with structured access to taxonomic classification depth, basin-level habitat distribution, peer-reviewed literature, and an interactive IUCN status matrix
concepts.description: CoralBase adopts an oceanographic-institute aesthetic: deep navy (#0A1628) headers contrast with white content panels, with coral-tinted accents (#E8572A) marking interactive elements. The Conservation Status Dashboard is the portal's analytical centerpiece — a matrix table cross-referencing taxonomic orders against all seven IUCN Red List categories, each cell showing a species count and capable of color-highlighting on click. Navigation uses persistent breadcrumbs and a mega-menu dropdown. Field guide entries follow a structured scholarly single-column format with named sections. Typography is clean and legible with clear hierarchy between headings and data labels.
concepts.motif: Deep navy (#0A1628) headers on white content panels; coral accent (#E8572A) on interactive affordances; IUCN category colors as a controlled semantic palette; structured scholarly typography with clear heading hierarchy; breadcrumb navigation and mega-menu dropdowns

pages.layout_pattern: full-width top mega-navigation bar with taxa-category dropdowns; hero feature banner with organism-of-the-week name, image placeholder, and conservation status pill; horizontal auto-scrolling ticker strip showing latest publication titles with journal and year; four equal-width quick-access category tiles (Taxonomy, Habitat Maps, Literature, Conservation) each with an icon, label, and record count

dual-panel: left 280px panel contains a searchable expand/collapse classification tree spanning phylum to species with indented hierarchy and node-count badges; right panel renders a two-column grid of taxonomy cards each showing a rank icon, scientific name in italics, authority citation, type locality, and a synonym count badge; selecting a tree node filters the right grid

vertically divided: upper half fills viewport width with a choropleth world map encoding species richness per ocean basin using a sequential blue color scale; lower half presents a tabular panel for the currently selected basin with columns for taxonomic order, species count, depth range, and endemic percentage; an unselected-state placeholder message prompts basin selection

full-width faceted search bar at top with keyword input and submit; below it a horizontal scrollable row of tag-filter chips grouped by Taxa, Year, and Journal with clear-all control; main content: paginated list of publication cards each containing title as linked heading, author byline, journal name, year badge, open-access icon where applicable, DOI link, and a two-sentence abstract excerpt with expand toggle

page-width status matrix table: sticky left column lists taxonomic orders; seven data columns represent IUCN categories (LC, NT, VU, EN, CR, EW, EX) each with a color-coded column header; cells show species counts; clicking a cell highlights it with its category color and opens an inline collapsible species roster as an in-table sub-row directly below the clicked row; summary totals row pinned to table bottom

fixed 200px alphabetical index sidebar on the left with A-Z letter anchors and species count per letter; main content area renders single-column scholarly field entries with clearly labeled H3 sections: Morphology, Habitat, Behavior, Diet, Reproduction, Conservation Notes; each entry includes a morphology diagram placeholder, a distribution thumbnail map, and a metadata strip with authority, family, and IUCN status

message_intent: communicate conservation urgency with immediate visual weight through the color-coded IUCN status matrix
provide taxonomic classification depth that supports rigorous academic citation and cross-referencing
connect basin-level distribution data to conservation status context for policy-relevant analysis
position portal as the evidence base for peer-reviewed marine conservation decision-making
content_model: hierarchical taxonomic classification records from kingdom to subspecies with authority, synonym list, and rank icons
ocean-basin choropleth geodata encoding species richness and endemic percentage per basin
scientific literature metadata with title, authors, journal, year, DOI, abstract excerpt, and taxa tags
IUCN Red List status matrix data cross-referencing taxonomic orders with all seven status categories
scholarly field guide entries with morphology, habitat, behavior, diet, reproduction, and conservation sections
interactions: click status matrix cell to color-highlight it and reveal an inline species roster beneath the table
expand and collapse taxonomy classification tree nodes from phylum to genus level
filter literature by faceted tag chips for taxa, year range, and journal name
click ocean basin polygon on choropleth map to load basin-specific species breakdown in lower panel
scroll alphabetical sidebar index to navigate directly to a field guide entry letter group

animations.description: Clicking a cell in the IUCN status matrix table triggers a CSS background-color transition on that cell from its neutral resting color (#F8F9FA light gray) to the IUCN category's designated color (e.g., #D32F2F deep red for CR, #E65100 amber for EN, #388E3C green for LC) over 400ms using a linear easing on the background-color property. The cell sits in its fixed table grid position throughout — no transform, no layout shift, no scroll, and no DOM replacement occur. The transition ends with the cell settled at the category highlight color, visually marking the selected state.

asset_needs: IUCN Red List category color tokens mapped to all seven categories (LC through EX)
ocean basin choropleth base SVG with labeled basin polygons
taxonomic rank icon set (phylum through species glyphs)
structured field guide entry layout template with named section anchors
concepts.mobile_behavior: status matrix becomes horizontally scrollable with frozen first column; mega-menu collapses to hamburger drawer; literature cards stack single column; field guide sidebar becomes a select dropdown
